title: Swim2Real: VLM-Guided System Identification for Sim-to-Real Transfer

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with sim-to-real transfer, system identification, VLM prompting, and RL, but the method itself is algorithmically straightforward
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that VLMs can guide system identification from video is interesting but not deeply surprising given recent VLM capabilities; marginal improvement over BayesOpt is somewhat expected
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Relies on proprietary VLM (Gemini 2.5 Pro) with version-specific behavior; custom hardware platform; prompts described but not fully specified; no code released
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Demonstrated hardware deployment on one platform; the automated calibration concept could reduce engineering effort for aquatic robotics labs, but proprietary VLM dependency limits adoption
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Only 5 seeds for calibration and 3 for RL; BayesOpt gap within variance; CMA-ES baseline unfairly constrained; limited real-world validation with open-loop execution
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Tested on single robot platform, single simulator, single VLM family; 16 parameters is modest; no evidence of transfer to other robotic systems or higher-dimensional spaces

title: ReviveMoE: Fast Recovery for Hardware Failures in Large-Scale MoE LLM Inference Deployments

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with distributed LLM serving, MoE architectures, and systems-level fault tolerance, but the individual techniques are well-established
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results are largely expected — avoiding unnecessary reinitializaton steps naturally reduces recovery time; the accuracy robustness to expert loss is somewhat expected given MoE redundancy
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Heavily dependent on proprietary Huawei infrastructure (Ascend NPUs, XCCL, xDeepServe, CloudMatrix384); impossible to reproduce outside Huawei's ecosystem
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Already deployed in Huawei Cloud's production MaaS; concepts are transferable to other cloud providers though implementation is platform-specific
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Single hardware configuration, single model, no variance reporting, no workload-level metrics; accuracy evaluation is more thorough but recovery time evaluation is thin
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Tested only on DeepSeek V3 on Ascend NPUs; concepts generalize but implementation is tightly coupled to Huawei's stack; only single-NPU failures addressed

title: From Skills to Talent: Organising Heterogeneous Agents as a Real-World Company

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with multi-agent systems, LLM agent frameworks, and basic formal methods (FSMs, DAGs), but the concepts are accessible to graduate-level ML researchers
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The organizational metaphor and its benefits are intuitive; the performance improvement is notable but not surprising given the use of multiple frontier models and added compute
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Open-source code, detailed architecture, and public benchmark used; however, reliance on proprietary API models (Claude, GPT-4o) and the Talent Market ecosystem introduces variability
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to industry teams building multi-agent LLM systems; the open-source framework with web UI is near-production ready
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Single benchmark without ablations, no cost-normalized comparisons, no statistical tests, no isolation of component contributions; case studies are qualitative only
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Architecture is domain-agnostic by design and case studies span four domains, but quantitative validation is limited to software development tasks only

title: Assessing the Potential of Masked Autoencoder Foundation Models in Predicting Downhole Metrics from Surface Drilling Data

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The paper requires only basic familiarity with ML concepts and drilling terminology; no advanced technical analysis is performed
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that foundation models haven't been applied to a niche industrial domain is entirely expected; no surprising conclusions emerge
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Search strings, databases, and inclusion criteria are well-specified, making the review process reproducible, though no empirical results exist to replicate
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The paper proposes an application direction but provides no evidence it works; actual translational value depends entirely on future empirical work
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: The central claim of MAEFM feasibility is supported only by analogy to two papers on different benchmarks; no direct evidence is provided
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Findings about the gap are specific to drilling; the broader observation that foundation models could help industrial time-series is somewhat generalizable but trivial

title: K-CARE: Knowledge-driven Symmetrical Contextual Anchoring and Analogical Prototype Reasoning for E-commerce Relevance

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with LLM fine-tuning, RAG, and e-commerce search systems, but individual components are well-established techniques
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that knowledge augmentation helps LLMs in specialized domains is expected; the specific diagnostic that 60% of failures are knowledge-related is mildly informative but not surprising
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Relies on proprietary JD.com search system, click logs, P2Q model, and GPT-5.2 for data synthesis; test data is not public; many implementation details are underspecified
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Already deployed on JD.com with demonstrated online improvements; directly applicable to other e-commerce platforms with similar infrastructure
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Large test set and online A/B test, but no statistical significance tests, limited baselines, modest improvements, and the online evaluation protocol has selection bias concerns
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Heavily tailored to e-commerce search with platform-specific components (search ranking signals, P2Q models, click logs); single-platform evaluation limits broader applicability claims

title: TacticGen: Grounding Adaptable and Scalable Generation of Football Tactics

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with diffusion models, multi-agent systems, and sports analytics, but individual components are well-established
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The results largely confirm expected behavior—guided generation improves tactical metrics, larger models perform better—with the 80% expert preference being the most notable finding
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Proprietary dataset from top-tier leagues is not publicly available; while architecture details and hyperparameters are provided, the data dependency is a major barrier to replication
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to professional football clubs for tactical planning and post-match analysis, with demonstrated expert validation from Birmingham City FC
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Comprehensive quantitative benchmarks with proper ablations and statistical tests in expert studies, but guidance effectiveness lacks outcome-based validation and direct comparison with closest competitors
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Demonstrated on football and basketball datasets, but heavily tailored to sports with synchronized tracking data; broader multi-agent applicability remains speculative

title: FASTER: Value-Guided Sampling for Fast RL

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level understanding of diffusion models and RL, but the method itself is surprisingly simple once the framework is understood.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that initial noise determines action quality is somewhat expected given prior work in image generation; the magnitude of compute savings is the main practical surprise.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code is released, hyperparameters are fully specified in tables, algorithms are clearly presented, and environments are standard benchmarks.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to VLA deployment in robotics; demonstrated on a 3.3B parameter model with significant inference speedups relevant to real-time control.
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Comprehensive experiments across settings and ablations, but lacking error bars/confidence intervals, real-world experiments, and theoretical grounding for the core assumption.
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Limited to diffusion/flow-based policy classes and tested only on manipulation tasks in simulation; the core insight may not transfer to all denoising schedules or domains.

title: Entanglement of multi-qubit quantum graph states and studies structural properties of tripartite graphs with quantum programming

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with quantum information theory, Pauli algebra, and graph theory, but the calculations themselves are straightforward expectation value computations
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results are a direct and expected extension of prior bipartite graph work; the structural connections follow naturally from the algebraic structure of the gates
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Analytical expressions are fully given and the quantum circuits are specified, though code is not provided and some notation errors may cause confusion
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: No quantum advantage over classical graph algorithms is demonstrated; claimed practical applications are entirely unsubstantiated
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Analytical derivations appear correct but numerical validation is limited to a single trivial 3-qubit example with no scaling or complexity analysis
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Framework is restricted to tripartite graphs; extension to general k-partite graphs is conceptually straightforward but not addressed

title: Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning with Runtime Safety Shielding for Power Grid Operation

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with reinforcement learning, power systems, and safety-constrained control, but the actual implementation is relatively straightforward
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results are entirely expected given the design — combining safety filtering with hierarchical control unsurprisingly outperforms components in isolation
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Grid2Op is public and some hyperparameters are given, but missing network sizes, reward function details, training duration, and code availability severely limit reproducibility
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The framework addresses real needs but the simplified action space and one-step lookahead limitation make real-world deployment premature
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: No external baselines, no error bars, missing figures, very small episode counts, and the large-grid ablation shows minimal differentiation between methods
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Zero-shot transfer is demonstrated but only across two Grid2Op environments with a drastically simplified action space; unclear if results hold with realistic action complexity

title: SafePilot: A Framework for Assuring LLM-enabled Cyber-Physical Systems

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with formal verification (FOL, LTL), LLM prompting, and CPS planning, but the individual components are well-established
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results are expected—iterative verification-guided prompting improves LLM plan quality, and hierarchical decomposition helps with complex tasks; no surprising findings
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Partial prompts shown, no code released, relies on proprietary GPT models, some implementation details missing (threshold values, complete prompt templates)
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Addresses a real need but demonstrated only on toy benchmarks; human expert requirement and lack of completeness guarantees limit practical deployment
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Only two toy benchmarks, no comparison with external baselines, no statistical analysis, no error bars despite stochastic LLM behavior, and 50 test problems per domain is limited
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Framework design is general in principle, but only demonstrated on discrete, deterministic planning problems; unclear applicability to continuous, stochastic, or real-time CPS

title: Quantum state transfer on a scalable network under unital and non-unital noise

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level understanding of quantum walks, graph theory, and open quantum systems, but the techniques employed are standard.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results are largely expected: noise degrades fidelity, non-unital noise is more damaging than unital noise, and state transfer quality varies with sender-receiver placement.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Operators and graph constructions are explicitly defined, but noise application protocol details are ambiguous, and no code is provided.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: No connection to physical implementations; butterfly graphs are not yet realized in any quantum hardware platform.
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Purely numerical with fixed parameters, no sensitivity analysis, limited time horizons, and no analytical proofs supporting the main claims.
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Results are specific to butterfly graphs from P₂ and P₃ seeds with particular noise parameters; no general principles are extracted.

title: Scaling Laws for Hybrid Quantum Neural Networks: Depth, Width, and Quantum-Centric Diagnostics

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The methodology involves standard quantum circuit simulation and classical ML training, accessible to graduate students with basic QML familiarity.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that width scales more reliably than depth is consistent with known barren plateau theory; no result is unexpected.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Architecture and hyperparameters are specified, PennyLane framework is identified, and datasets are public, but no code is provided and single-run results limit verification.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Performance is far below classical baselines; no pathway to practical deployment is demonstrated.
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Single-run experiments without error bars, no classical baselines, and narrow parameter ranges make most quantitative claims unreliable.
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Three image classification datasets are tested, but only one circuit ansatz, one encoding scheme, and a very narrow scale range are explored.

title: TrinityGuard: A Unified Framework for Safeguarding Multi-Agent Systems

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The framework engineering is competent but conceptually accessible; requires familiarity with MAS and LLM safety but no deep specialist expertise
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that MAS are broadly vulnerable is expected; the extremely low pass rates across all risk types confirm prior intuitions rather than overturning assumptions
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code is released on GitHub and the architecture is well-described, though judge prompts, test case details, and LLM configurations lack sufficient specificity
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to MAS developers and security teams; OWASP alignment and open-source release facilitate industry adoption
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: No judge validation, no baselines, no statistical analysis, synthetic evaluation raises ecological validity concerns, and near-zero pass rates are uninterpretable without judge calibration
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Claims platform-agnostic design but only demonstrates on AG2; synthetic workflows cover seven domains but real-world applicability remains unproven

title: Causal Scene Narration with Runtime Safety Supervision for Vision-Language-Action Driving

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with VLA models, preference optimization, and autonomous driving evaluation but no deeply specialized theoretical knowledge
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that text structure matters beyond information content is intuitive but the 39.1% decomposition quantifies it usefully; TTC degradation finding is genuinely surprising
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Uses open-source CARLA and LMDrive; evaluation protocol well-specified with seeds and CIs; code promised but not yet released
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Currently simulation-only with privileged data; real-world deployment requires perception pipeline integration that remains unvalidated
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Clean ablation design but N=5 is small, single architecture limits generalizability, key comparisons have overlapping CIs, and noise ablation results are slightly counterintuitive
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Single VLA architecture, single simulator, privileged data—claims of architecture-agnostic applicability are entirely untested

title: FairQE: Multi-Agent Framework for Mitigating Gender Bias in Translation Quality Estimation

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The framework combines prompt engineering with straightforward score aggregation; requires familiarity with QE and MT evaluation but no deep theoretical expertise
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that combining LLM reasoning with contrastive variants reduces gender bias is intuitive rather than surprising; the lack of fairness-accuracy tradeoff is mildly surprising
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Full prompts are provided (a strength), but reliance on proprietary GPT-4.1-mini API limits exact reproducibility; no code released
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to MT evaluation pipelines in production settings, though API costs and latency may limit scalability
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Multiple evaluation settings are covered but no statistical significance tests, limited failure analysis, no human evaluation, and some overcorrection issues are not fully addressed
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Only English-source pairs tested, binary gender framework, and reliance on specific LLM behavior limits generalization to other source languages and broader gender concepts

title: From Code to Figure: A FAIR-Aligned Data Provenance Chain for Reproducible Simulation Research in Numerical Physics

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The concepts are standard software engineering practices; accessible to anyone with basic familiarity with version control, CI/CD, and HDF5.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: No unexpected findings; the paper documents the application of well-known best practices without revealing surprising outcomes.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: The workflow is described with code snippets and tool names, but the monstr framework and analysis library are not publicly released, limiting direct replication.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The practices described are directly applicable to other computational research groups, though no reusable tools are provided to facilitate adoption.
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: No quantitative evaluation, user studies, or comparative analysis; claims of effectiveness rest entirely on description of the approach.
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Claims of broad applicability are plausible but undemonstrated; the workflow is shown only for one C++ framework in one physics subdomain.

title: See Something, Say Something: Context-Criticality-Aware Mobile Robot Communication for Hazard Mitigations

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The technical components are off-the-shelf (BLIP, GPT-4, Grad-CAM, ROS); the main challenge is systems integration rather than algorithmic innovation
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that context-aware communication outperforms context-free alerts is entirely expected; no surprising results emerge
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Prompt is provided and model versions specified, but user study details are insufficient (no N reported), hardware is custom, and cloud API dependency limits exact replication
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The concept is applicable to real-world robot deployment but 12s latency, cloud dependency, and unresolved safety guarantees prevent near-term safety-critical use
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: No statistical tests, unknown participant count, trivially weak baseline, only 5 scenarios with ~12 runs each, and the optimization objective is never actually optimized
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Only 5 indoor single-hazard scenarios tested in one building; no multi-hazard, outdoor, crowded, or temporally evolving situations evaluated

title: Zero-Uncertainty States Relative to Observable Algebras

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with C*-algebras, representation theory (Artin-Wedderburn), and quantum information theory (Stinespring, Choi-Jamiołkowski)
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The results—purity and maximal entanglement forced by full algebra generation—align well with intuition; the main value is in the formal proof rather than unexpected conclusions
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: All proofs are fully detailed with standard techniques; no computational experiments to reproduce; results are purely mathematical and verifiable
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The steering interpretation has conceptual relevance but the paper does not develop concrete protocols or applications beyond the theoretical characterization
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Mathematical proofs are complete and correct for the claims made, but the scope is limited by the acknowledged incompleteness of the classification
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Framework is restricted to finite-dimensional systems and projective measurements; extension to POVMs, infinite dimensions, or approximate ZUS is not addressed

title: Synthetic data in cryptocurrencies using generative models

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Uses standard deep learning components (LSTM, CGAN, MLP) with well-known training procedures; accessible to anyone with basic deep learning knowledge
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Near-perfect correlation results are expected given the autoregressive minute-level conditioning setup; no surprising findings emerge
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Algorithm is specified and hyperparameters listed, but no code is released and the LSEG data source may require paid access
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: No downstream application demonstrated; crypto price data is already public, undermining the privacy motivation
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Evaluation metrics (Pearson correlation) are inappropriate for the task; no baselines, no distributional tests, no downstream utility evaluation
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Only tested on three cryptocurrencies with a single architecture; no evidence the approach generalizes to other financial instruments or data types

title: StructBiHOI: Structured Articulation Modeling for Long--Horizon Bimanual Hand--Object Interaction Generation

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with diffusion models, VAEs, state-space models, and hand-object interaction representations
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The results are largely expected—hierarchical decomposition improves over monolithic approaches, and Mamba outperforms Transformers on long sequences, both predictable outcomes
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Dataset is public (ARCTIC) but custom splits are used, no code is released, and some training details (learning rates, loss weights) are missing
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Relevant to robotic manipulation and animation but lacks physics validation or real-world deployment, limiting immediate applicability
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Ablations are provided but no error bars, limited baselines, narrow metric set, single dataset evaluation, and no statistical significance testing
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Only evaluated on ARCTIC dataset variants; generalization to diverse object categories, different hand models, or real-world settings is untested

title: Path Integral Approach to Quantum Fisher Information

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with path integrals, Schwinger-Keldysh formalism, semiclassical approximations, and quantum estimation theory
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results are formal rewritings of known expressions; no unexpected physical or mathematical insights emerge
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Derivations are fully detailed with intermediate steps; purely analytical work that can be followed and verified
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Potential applications to sensing and metrology are mentioned but no concrete path to implementation is demonstrated
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: No numerical examples, no worked applications, no comparisons with alternative methods; all claims are formal/structural
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Formalism is general in principle but restricted to pure states and unitary evolution; field-theoretic extension is formal without concrete demonstrations

title: diffpy.morph: Python tools for model independent comparisons between sets of 1D functions

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with PDF analysis and diffraction methods, but the mathematical content is accessible to graduate students in materials science or condensed matter physics
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results confirm expected physical behavior (thermal expansion, phase transitions) using standard mathematical transformations; no unexpected findings
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Open-source code on PyPI/conda-forge/GitHub, real datasets from published studies, detailed mathematical specifications of all transformations, and documentation with tutorials
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to routine analysis at synchrotron/XFEL/neutron facilities and could be integrated into automated data processing pipelines
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Multiple real-world examples with cross-validation against PDFgui, but no error bars on extracted parameters, no statistical significance testing, and no systematic benchmarking
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Claimed to work on any 1D function but only demonstrated on diffraction patterns and PDFs; physical morphs are specific to crystallographic data

title: Jellyfish: Zero-Shot Federated Unlearning Scheme with Knowledge Disentanglement

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with federated learning, knowledge distillation, and multi-objective optimization, but individual components are well-established
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results confirm that combining multiple known techniques works; no unexpected findings or counterintuitive insights emerge
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code is provided, algorithms are detailed, and hyperparameters are specified, though some implementation details for baselines in federated settings could be clearer
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Addresses a real regulatory need (GDPR compliance) but limited to CNN-based image classification, restricting immediate deployment to narrow use cases
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Experiments cover multiple datasets and baselines with ablations and MIA evaluation, but lack non-IID settings, scalability tests, larger datasets, and statistical significance reporting
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Restricted to CNN architectures and small-scale image classification; no evaluation on non-IID distributions, other modalities, or modern architectures like transformers

title: A Dynamical Equilibrium Linking Nanohertz Stochastic Gravitational Wave Background to Cosmic Structure Formation

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires expertise in both non-equilibrium statistical mechanics and general relativistic perturbation theory, plus familiarity with PTA data analysis and cosmological structure formation
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The claim that SGWB back-reaction is cosmologically significant directly contradicts the standard assumption in the field, and the numerical coincidence with MNL is unexpected
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: The PTA analysis uses standard public tools and datasets; the theoretical framework is described in detail but some derivation steps require careful reconstruction
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Primarily theoretical with potential observational tests via future surveys, but no immediate practical applications
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: The Bayes factor comparison is technically adequate but the physical foundations are insufficiently justified; the mc-MNL coincidence spans two orders of magnitude and could be accidental
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: If valid, the framework would apply broadly to cosmic structure formation, but currently tested only against one PTA dataset with no independent verification of the screening mechanism

title: DeepFRI Demystified: Interpretability vs. Accuracy in AI Protein Function Prediction

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with GCNs, protein biology, and XAI methods, but the applied techniques are well-documented and straightforward to implement
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that mutating the most 'relevant' residues doesn't change predictions is somewhat surprising and challenges assumptions about GradCAM reliability in this context
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: No code released, hardcoded dissimilar amino acids not specified, protein selection criteria vague, and key experimental details are underspecified
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Raises awareness about XAI limitations in protein function prediction but provides no concrete tools or validated improvements for practitioners
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Claims based on two case studies despite 124 proteins tested; no statistical tests, no ground truth benchmarking, and key quantitative claims are unsupported
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Only tested on binding proteins with one model (DeepFRI); detailed results shown for only two proteins; unclear if findings extend to other protein classes or function prediction tools

title: Meta-Learned Basis Adaptation for Parametric Linear PDEs

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with physics-informed neural networks, extreme learning machines, meta-learning, and radial basis function methods, but the mathematical formulation is accessible to graduate students in computational science
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The results largely confirm expected behavior—that task-adaptive basis placement outperforms uniform grids—rather than revealing unexpected phenomena
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code release promised but not yet available; corrector construction involves many problem-specific choices not fully detailed; main predictor architecture is reasonably specified
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Limited to linear PDEs with low-dimensional parameterizations; significant extensions needed for real-world scientific computing applications
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Ablations are well-designed but no statistical reporting, no comparison with classical reduced-basis methods, and error levels remain modest in several cases
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Each PDE family requires custom ansatz, regularization, and corrector design; restricted to linear PDEs in 1D-2D with 2-3 parameters; unclear how the approach scales

title: Signature Placement in Post-Quantum TLS Certificate Hierarchies: An Experimental Study of ML-DSA and SLH-DSA in TLS 1.3 Authentication

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with TLS 1.3, X.509 PKI, and post-quantum cryptography standards, but no deep mathematical expertise
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The main finding — that SLH-DSA's slow signing dominates when placed at the leaf — is largely predictable from known primitive-level benchmarks; the chain-exposure depth finding is mildly surprising
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Scenario construction is well-documented with specific algorithm choices and OpenSSL/oqsprovider versions, but no code is released and some execution details are underspecified
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The hierarchy-sensitive deployment recommendations are directly applicable to organizations planning post-quantum PKI migration
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Regime-level differences are convincing due to orders-of-magnitude separation, but lack of statistical tests, single parameter set, single stack, and no error bars weaken confidence in finer-grained claims
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Single implementation stack, single SLH-DSA parameter set, localhost-only execution, and absence of other PQ signature families significantly limit generalizability

title: Multi-stage Planning for Multi-target Surveillance using Aircrafts Equipped with Synthetic Aperture Radars Aware of Target Visibility

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with RL, path planning, TSP formulations, and SAR imaging constraints, but individual components are well-known
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results are largely expected: backbone saturation, Transformer TSP being competitive, A* performing well for trajectory generation—no unexpected findings
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Algorithm descriptions are provided and standard components are used, but no code/data release and some implementation details (terrain dataset, visibility map generation) are underspecified
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly addresses a practical SAR mission planning need, but lacks obstacle avoidance, dynamic replanning, and real-world validation needed for deployment
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Extensive algorithmic comparisons but no real-world validation, no SAR image quality assessment, no joint optimization baseline, and the segment prediction evaluation appears to saturate trivially
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Evaluated only on natural terrain with static targets; excludes urban environments, obstacles, dynamic scenarios, and multi-aircraft coordination

title: Learning Bimanual Cloth Manipulation with Vision-based Tactile Sensing via Single Robotic Arm

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level knowledge of robotic manipulation, tactile sensing, and vision transformers, but individual components are standard
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The results largely confirm that tactile sensing helps cloth manipulation and that synthetic data improves learning — both expected outcomes. Success rates are modest.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Custom hardware gripper makes full replication difficult; perception code and model details are reasonably specified but no code release mentioned; supplementary site referenced
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Addresses a real need for simpler cloth manipulation systems, but current success rates (57-69%) are below deployment thresholds; limited to small cloths
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Perception evaluation is reasonable with baselines and ablations, but real-world experiments are limited (70 trials, no error bars, no statistical tests, constrained initial conditions)
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Tested on 7 fabrics of varying patterns which is decent, but cloth sizes are small, the starting condition is constrained, and scaling to real garments is undemonstrated

title: The second altermagnet candidate in organic conductors: $κ$-(BEDT-TTF)$_2$$m$-HOOCC$_6$H$_4$SO$_3$

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level knowledge of organic conductors, Mott physics, frustrated magnetism, and altermagnetism concepts
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The AFM order in a near-equilateral triangular lattice is genuinely surprising, though the altermagnetic candidacy follows directly from prior theory
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Synthesis conditions provided but disorder complicates reproducibility; computational parameters partially specified but some choices appear ad hoc
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Organic altermagnets could eventually impact spintronics, but this is far from application — no experimental confirmation of altermagnetic functionality
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Experimental evidence for canted AFM is convincing, but the altermagnet claim rests on theoretical analogy and mean-field calculations only; t'/t inconsistency weakens the narrative
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Suggests altermagnetism may be more general in κ-type salts but limited to one new compound; the role of anion dipoles may not transfer broadly

title: You're Pushing My Buttons: Instrumented Learning of Gentle Button Presses

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The methods combine existing components (AST, Diffusion Policy, ResNet) in a straightforward way; accessible to graduate students in robotics/ML
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results largely align with expectations—privileged information helps, but the modest improvement and failure of Soft Sensor are somewhat predictable
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Custom hardware required, no code/data released, dataset size unspecified, though the pipeline is described at a high level
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The paradigm of training-time instrumentation could be practical for manufacturing/assembly tasks, but validation is too preliminary

new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The idea of using PA distortions as physical triggers is creative, though the high ASR results are somewhat expected given the controlled simulation setup

new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The threat model requires transmitter control and lacks over-the-air validation, making real-world applicability uncertain but the concept has security assessment value
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Results rely on simplified PA model, single architecture, no error bars or statistical significance tests, and superficial defense evaluation without comprehensive ablations
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Only tested with one PA model, one DL architecture, one dataset configuration; generalization to realistic PAs, diverse architectures, and real-world conditions is undemonstrated

title: DexDrummer: In-Hand, Contact-Rich, and Long-Horizon Dexterous Robot Drumming

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with sim-to-real RL, dexterous manipulation, and contact dynamics, though individual components are well-established
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The results are largely expected — hierarchical decomposition with contact rewards works for this task, and reactive grasp outperforms fixed grasp as anticipated
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code is released, ManiSkill environment is specified, and training details are provided, though real-world setup specifics (camera calibration, controller tuning) are sparse
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The specific drumming application has limited commercial value, but insights about contact-rich tool manipulation could inform assembly or other industrial tasks
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: No error bars or multi-seed evaluation, real-world experiments are limited to very short sequences, and key ablations are described only qualitatively via videos
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Heavy task-specific reward engineering, single hardware platform, and drumming-specific design choices limit transferability to other contact-rich manipulation tasks

title: LessMimic: Long-Horizon Humanoid Interaction with Unified Distance Field Representations

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires expertise in RL, whole-body humanoid control, adversarial training, and distance field representations—a challenging intersection of subfields
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The DF representation's effectiveness for scale generalization is somewhat expected given its mathematical properties, though the degree of long-horizon composition capability is noteworthy
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Architecture and training details are provided with hyperparameters, but relies on specific simulation infrastructure, retargeted MoCap data, and a physical humanoid platform; no code release mentioned
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Real-world demonstrations exist but are limited; the root trajectory requirement and vision pipeline degradation create gaps before practical deployment
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Simulation results are systematic with ablations, but only 3 seeds, limited real-world trials (10 per condition), and missing comparisons for long-horizon composition weaken evidence
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Tested on 4 relatively simple tasks with rigid objects; extension to articulated/deformable objects, cluttered scenes, and diverse manipulation tasks is untested

title: Quantum Finite Temperature Lanczos Method

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with quantum Krylov methods, trace estimation, and Trotterization, but the individual components are well-established.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The results largely confirm expected behavior: Trotter errors dominate at low T, typicality helps at high T, and noise sensitivity is severe. No unexpected findings.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code and data are publicly available, algorithm is fully specified with pseudocode, and the benchmark model is standard.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The extreme precision requirements (10^-6 on overlaps) and lack of resource estimates make near-term quantum hardware implementation impractical.
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Systematic analysis of error sources on one model, but only a single simple benchmark, no comparison with competing methods, and no demonstration beyond classically simulable sizes.
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Only tested on the 1D TFIM; applicability to frustrated, fermionic, or higher-dimensional systems is claimed but entirely undemonstrated.

title: VRUD: A Drone Dataset for Complex Vehicle-VRU Interactions within Mixed Traffic

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The technical pipeline uses standard tools (YOLO11, ByteTrack, RTS smoothing) and the VTTC metric is a straightforward extension; accessible to graduate students in autonomous driving
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that vehicles drive more conservatively with higher VRU density is expected; the 0.7s VTTC convergence is mildly interesting but not deeply surprising
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Pipeline is described at a high level with standard tools, but missing detection/tracking metrics, hyperparameters for threshold selection, and no code for the processing pipeline is mentioned
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to robotaxi development and testing in unstructured urban environments, particularly in Chinese cities
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Validation lacks quantitative error metrics, no detection/tracking performance reported, no downstream task benchmarking, and statistical analyses are largely descriptive
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Only 2 sites in one city; urban village characteristics may not transfer to other regions or countries; limited temporal coverage

title: Fault-Tolerant Cut-Cat State Syndrome Extraction for Quantum Codes

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level expertise in quantum error correction, stabilizer formalism, and fault-tolerant protocols, with detailed case-by-case analysis of error propagation
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The hybrid approach between cat states and flag qubits is a natural interpolation; the resource savings are notable but not fundamentally surprising
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Algorithms are explicitly specified and proofs are detailed, but no code is provided and some implementation details (adaptive strategies, LUT construction) require careful reconstruction
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to fault-tolerant quantum computing implementations using CSS codes with high-weight stabilizers, relevant for near-term hardware
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Formal proofs for d≤7, numerical validation matching expected scaling, and resource comparisons are provided, but threshold analysis is missing and d=9 relies on exhaustive enumeration rather than proof
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Limited to CSS codes, demonstrated only up to d=9, with arbitrary-distance extension only conjectured; noise model restricted to depolarizing

title: MonoEM-GS: Monocular Expectation-Maximization Gaussian Splatting SLAM

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level understanding of probabilistic inference, Gaussian Splatting, ICP registration, and monocular SLAM systems
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The improved geometric consistency is expected given the EM formulation; the magnitude of pose accuracy degradation relative to baselines is somewhat surprising
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Parameters are specified and the pipeline is described in detail, but no code is released and some implementation details (e.g., clustering, voxelization) lack specifics
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Multi-modal maps with semantic features and clean meshes are directly useful for robotic navigation and manipulation, though room-scale limitation constrains applicability
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Competitive quantitative results but thin margins, no ablation studies, no statistical significance tests, no runtime analysis, and weaker pose estimation undermines SLAM claims
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Tested on three indoor datasets only, limited to room-scale, depends on specific foundation model, and every-10th-frame processing may not generalize to real-time scenarios

title: Radar Odometry Subject to High Tilt Dynamics of Subarctic Environments

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with radar signal processing, ICP-based registration, and IMU sensor fusion, but the individual components are well-established
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that tilt-aware processing helps in high-tilt conditions is expected; the degree of degradation of existing methods is mildly informative but not surprising
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Pipeline is well-described with parameters tabulated; uses ROS2 and libpointmatcher; however, code is not released and the FoMo dataset is from the same group
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to mining and forestry robot navigation in subarctic terrain, though the modest improvement may not justify deployment changes
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: No ablation study, no statistical significance tests, marginal 0.3% improvement, and missing comparison with RINO; 20 total runs provide some breadth but insufficient depth
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Evaluated on single dataset from one group, one radar sensor, one platform; unclear how findings transfer to other rough terrain types or radar configurations

title: Gust Estimation and Rejection with a Disturbance Observer for Proprioceptive Underwater Soft Morphing Wings

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with soft robotics modeling (PCC), thin airfoil theory, and state estimation (EKF), but the individual components are well-established
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The idea that a flexible wing deforms under flow disturbances and this deformation can be used for estimation is intuitive rather than surprising; the results confirm expected behavior
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Model equations are provided in detail and parameters are specified, but EKF tuning is manual, some experimental data comes from prior work, and no code is released
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Promising concept for soft underwater vehicles but far from deployment; simulation-only control validation and single-DOF limitation require significant further development
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Dynamic model validated against limited experimental data; observer and controller validated only in simulation with idealized disturbances; no noise robustness analysis or statistical rigor
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Single DOF, single flow speed, idealized disturbances, and heavy reliance on corrective mappings fitted to sparse data limit generalization to real-world conditions

title: NetSecBed: A Container-Native Testbed for Reproducible Cybersecurity Experimentation

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires basic familiarity with Docker, network security, and testbed design; no advanced theoretical or algorithmic challenges
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results are entirely expected — SYN floods degrade HTTP service, containers work as isolating mechanisms — no surprising findings
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Repository is anonymized/unavailable; only 1 of 60 scenarios demonstrated; hardware specs provided but no reproducibility package accessible
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Direct practical utility for cybersecurity researchers and educators needing controlled dataset generation, if fully released and documented
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Only 1 of 60 attacks tested, 1 of 9 services evaluated, no statistical analysis, no downstream validation, no scalability testing — claims vastly exceed evidence
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Claims generality across IoT/IIoT/CPS but only demonstrates basic HTTP SYN flood; no evidence that framework handles protocol-specific complexities at scale

title: Clawed and Dangerous: Can We Trust Open Agentic Systems?

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with software engineering lifecycle concepts and LLM agent architectures, but no deep technical specialization needed
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The gaps identified (memory integrity, supply chain, operational governance) are somewhat expected; the value is in systematic quantification rather than unexpected findings
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Search queries and screening criteria are detailed, but single-coder taxonomy coding without inter-rater reliability limits reproducibility of the synthesis
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The taxonomy, scorecard, and doctrine are directly applicable to agent platform design and security auditing, though the doctrine lacks empirical validation
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Quantitative gap analysis is well-grounded but the reference doctrine is unvalidated, corpus is preprint-heavy, and single-coder limitation weakens synthesis reliability
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Taxonomy and principles apply broadly to open agentic systems beyond the specific examples, but acknowledged limitations for browser, mobile, and embodied agents

title: ProgressVLA: Progress-Guided Diffusion Policy for Vision-Language Robotic Manipulation

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with diffusion models, VLA architectures, world models, and classifier guidance techniques
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that explicit progress guidance improves long-horizon manipulation is intuitive and expected; the magnitude of improvement is moderate
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Architecture details are provided but rely on multiple pretrained components (UniVLA, DiTA, DINOv2, CLIP); no code released; real-world setup is specific
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Progress-based termination and guidance are directly applicable to deployed robotic systems; real-world experiments demonstrate feasibility
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Multiple benchmarks tested with ablations, but no error bars, limited real-world trials (20/task), and some comparisons use different input modalities
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Tested on two simulation benchmarks and real-world, but progress proxy assumes monotonic demonstrations and world model quality may limit transfer to diverse domains

title: Category-based and Popularity-guided Video Game Recommendation: A Balance-oriented Framework

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with GNN-based recommender systems and BPR loss, but the individual components are standard techniques combined in a domain-specific manner
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results are largely expected—stricter game connections improve accuracy, diverse connections improve diversity, and combining both yields a trade-off. No surprising findings emerge.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code and data are released, hyperparameters are specified, but some implementation details are in appendix and statistical significance is not reported
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to Steam-like gaming platforms, though the specific category structure limits immediate deployment to other domains
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Single dataset evaluation with no significance tests, modest improvements over baselines, and case studies that are illustrative but not rigorous
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Framework is tightly coupled to video game-specific categories (genre, developer, publisher); unclear how it transfers to other recommendation domains or datasets

title: Silence and Noise: Self-censorship and Opinion Expression on Social Media

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The methods are standard social science approaches (surveys, logistic regression, thematic analysis) accessible to graduate students in HCI/CSCW
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Core findings—that larger audiences and lower perceived support increase self-censorship—largely confirm existing theoretical predictions from Spiral of Silence and social identity theory
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Survey instrument is provided in the appendix and statistical methods are described, but interview codebooks and raw data are not shared
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Design implications for platform features (graduated audience controls, pre-posting nudges) are actionable but untested
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Effect sizes are small, Model 2 has mostly non-significant predictors, mediation effects are borderline, and self-report data introduces systematic uncertainty for studying concealment behavior
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: U.S.-only, English-speaking, Prolific-recruited sample with demographic skews; six researcher-selected topics; findings may not transfer to other cultural or platform contexts

title: Digital Twin-Assisted Measurement Design and Channel Statistics Prediction

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with Gaussian processes, ray tracing, and information-theoretic sensor placement, but no deep specialized expertise
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The result that geometry-aware priors from uncalibrated DTs outperform stationary kernels is intuitive and expected; the magnitude of improvement is the modest surprise
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Scene setup, DT parameters, and GP equations are specified; Sionna RT is publicly available; however, some implementation details (noise variance, exact OpenStreetMap region) and code are not provided
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to network planning and URLLC deployment using freely available tools (OpenStreetMap, Sionna), though real-world validation is missing
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Single scenario evaluation with simulation-only validation and relatively weak baselines; no statistical significance tests, no real measurements, and limited ablation studies
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Tested only on one urban scene with one AP; unclear how performance varies across different environments, frequencies, or deployment configurations

title: On Online Control of Opinion Dynamics

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level knowledge of adaptive control, Lyapunov analysis, and opinion dynamics models, though individual components are standard
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results are largely expected: alternating estimation and control converges, and knowing parameters yields better performance than estimating them online
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Network topology, size, and specific simulation parameters are not fully specified; no code provided; key hyperparameter choices (c_δ, γ, α_min) lack concrete guidance
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Addresses a real problem in influence campaigns but strong assumptions (known V, deterministic dynamics, single scalar susceptibility) limit direct real-world deployment
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Theoretical claims are supported by proofs, but experimental evidence is thin—single network configuration, no error bars, limited baseline comparisons, no real data
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Restricted to the specific FJ-based model with known V, strongly connected graphs, and deterministic dynamics; unclear how results extend to more general opinion formation models or heterogeneous targets

title: Humanizing Robot Gaze Shifts: A Framework for Natural Gaze Shifts in Humanoid Robots

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with VLMs, VQ-VAEs, gaze kinematics, and HRI design, but individual components are well-established
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: Results are largely expected — VLMs can reason about social cues, and VQ-VAEs can model diverse discrete modes. No surprising findings emerge.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Self-collected unreleased dataset, commercial VLM dependency, custom robot platform, and insufficient detail on perception model specifics limit reproducibility
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly targets a real application in social robotics, but lacks user study validation needed before deployment
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: No baseline comparisons, no user studies, very small dataset, isolated module evaluation, and qualitative-only diversity assessment
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Tested on a single robot platform with very limited scenarios; only four gaze regularities evaluated with few clips each; 805-sample training set limits generalization claims

title: A reduced-cost two-component relativistic equation-of-motion coupled cluster method for the double electron attachment problem

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires deep expertise in relativistic quantum chemistry, coupled-cluster theory, and integral decomposition techniques — a narrow specialist intersection
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The results are largely expected: X2CAMF matches 4c, SS-FNS outperforms FNS, and computed properties agree with prior theoretical work within known error bars
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: In-house BAGH code not publicly available; while theoretical framework is well-described, practical replication would require substantial independent implementation effort
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Applicable to computational studies of heavy-element chemistry but several steps removed from direct experimental or industrial application
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Good agreement with experiment and prior theory, but missing computational cost data undermines the central efficiency claim; limited to small systems
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Demonstrated only for atoms and diatomics; transferability to polyatomic systems, which would most benefit from cost reduction, is not established

title: Experience Compression Spectrum: Unifying Memory, Skills, and Rules in LLM Agents

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The paper is accessible to anyone familiar with LLM agents; the formal framework is straightforward and the main contribution is conceptual rather than technically demanding.
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The community disconnect finding is somewhat surprising, but the core insight that memory/skills/rules form a spectrum is relatively intuitive once stated.
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: The citation analysis could be reproduced given the 22 papers, but compression ratios and system mappings involve subjective judgment. No code or experiments to replicate.
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The framework directly addresses production LLM agent deployment challenges, but no implementation exists to demonstrate practical value.
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Claims rest on citation counting, approximate compression ratios from heterogeneous sources, and aggregation of others' benchmarks with acknowledged confounds. No original experiments.
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: The framework is intentionally broad and applicable across agent domains, though validated only on text-based systems and the discrete four-level taxonomy may not generalize cleanly.

title: "Why This Avoidance Maneuver?" Contrastive Explanations in Human-Supervised Maritime Autonomous Navigation

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with maritime collision avoidance, multi-objective optimization, and XAI concepts, but the technical methods are straightforward
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that explanations help in complex scenarios but add workload in simple ones is largely intuitive; no results challenge existing assumptions
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: The framework and CAS are well-specified with references to existing open simulation tools, though the visualization interface and study materials would need recreation
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly targets a real operational need in maritime autonomy, but significant further development and validation needed before deployment
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Only 4 participants, no control condition, no statistical analysis, no performance metrics—claims are largely supported by anecdotal qualitative feedback
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Framework requires linearly decomposable cost functions, tested on single CAS type and small vessel; unclear how findings transfer to larger vessels, different planners, or real operational conditions

title: Automated near-term quantum algorithm discovery for molecular ground states

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level understanding of both VQE algorithms in quantum chemistry and LLM-driven program synthesis frameworks
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The discovered mechanisms largely recapitulate known heuristics (MP2 scoring, coordinate descent, pruning); the main surprise is the magnitude of resource reduction achievable by combining them
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Skeleton code and final algorithms are released, but the Hive platform is proprietary, making the discovery process itself non-reproducible
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Demonstrates methodology applicable to quantum algorithm design, but tested only on classically trivial molecules; real-world utility requires scaling to larger systems
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Three molecules with ablations are solid, but minimal basis set, single hardware data point with marginal significance, and limited baseline comparisons weaken the evidence
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Discovered algorithms generalize across bond lengths for a given molecule, but molecule-specific codes and small system sizes leave broader generalization undemonstrated

title: Benchmarking Quantum Kernel Support Vector Machines Against Classical Baselines on Tabular Data: A Rigorous Empirical Study with Hardware Validation

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level familiarity with quantum computing, kernel methods, and experimental design, but no deep specialist expertise in any single area
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The negative result for quantum kernels on small tabular datasets is broadly expected given prior theoretical work on exponential concentration and kernel inductive bias
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Complete codebase with 18,700 lines, 683 cached kernel matrices, 132 unit tests, Zenodo archive, and detailed experimental specifications
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: The negative finding discourages near-term deployment of quantum kernels for tabular classification; the spectral diagnostic criterion has modest practical utility for researchers
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: 970 experiments with nested CV, multiple statistical tests, hardware validation, and seed sensitivity analysis; limited only by small qubit counts and dataset scope
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Results limited to 9 UCI datasets, binary classification, 3-8 qubits, and 4 feature maps; conclusions may not transfer to structured or higher-dimensional quantum advantage regimes

title: Noise-enhanced quantum kernels on analog quantum computers

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level knowledge of quantum computing, open quantum systems, and kernel methods, but the mathematical tools are standard in these fields
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The noise-enhancement finding is counterintuitive and moderately surprising, though noise-as-regularization has precedent in classical ML and some quantum contexts
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Key parameters and models are specified, QuantumToolbox.jl is referenced, but no code/data is released and some details (hyperparameter tuning, PCA specifics) are underspecified
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Could inform practical quantum kernel implementations on Rydberg platforms, but requires hardware validation and scaling studies before deployment
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: No error bars or statistical tests, single-run results, small datasets, and the noise-enhancement mechanism remains conjectural rather than rigorously demonstrated
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Tested on only two tasks (one synthetic, one physics-specific), with a single noise model and limited parameter exploration; unclear how findings transfer to other data types or noise regimes

title: HiPolicy: Hierarchical Multi-Frequency Action Chunking for Policy Learning

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires familiarity with diffusion-based policies, action chunking, and hierarchical architectures, but the individual components are well-established
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The multi-frequency decomposition idea is intuitive and the improvements, while substantial, are in the expected direction; the entropy-execution link is somewhat predictable
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Hyperparameters are specified and architecture details are provided, but code is not explicitly released; entropy threshold selection procedure may be hard to replicate for new tasks
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Plug-and-play integration with existing policies and real-world demonstrations on a standard robot arm suggest near-term practical applicability
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Good breadth of experiments but missing comparisons with competing hierarchical methods, limited statistical analysis (10 real-world trials, no confidence intervals), and ablations on only one benchmark
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Evaluated only on RoboTwin benchmarks (single simulator family) and one robot platform; fixed entropy thresholds and hand-designed frequency levels may not transfer well

title: Strain-Parameterized Coupled Dynamics and Dual-Camera Visual Servoing for Aerial Continuum Manipulators

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires graduate-level expertise in Lie group dynamics, continuum robot modeling, visual servoing, and adaptive control theory
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The results are largely expected—the proposed improvements yield incremental performance gains consistent with the theoretical framework, without overturning existing assumptions
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Physical parameters and formulations are provided, but key implementation details (gain tuning, solver specifics, code) are missing; custom hardware makes exact replication difficult
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly relevant to aerial manipulation applications such as inspection and maintenance, though near-hover limitations and oscillation issues require further resolution before deployment
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Simulation and experimental results demonstrate feasibility but lack statistical rigor, repeated trials, baseline comparisons for control performance, and testing under diverse conditions
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Framework is theoretically generalizable to multi-section CRs and different UAV platforms, but only demonstrated on a single-section CR under limited conditions

title: Mind Your HEARTBEAT! Claw Background Execution Inherently Enables Silent Memory Pollution

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires understanding of agent architectures and memory systems but the concepts are accessible to anyone familiar with LLM agents
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The architectural vulnerability is somewhat intuitive in hindsight, but the finding that prompt injection is unnecessary and that ordinary social misinformation suffices is genuinely surprising
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: MissClaw is described in detail and built on open-source Moltbook stack; agent configurations and prompts are provided in appendices, though code is not released
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly applicable to deployed agent frameworks; findings should immediately inform design decisions for persistent agent architectures
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Systematic experiments across three studies but lacking statistical tests, confidence intervals, and testing on only one model family with modest sample sizes
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: The architectural vulnerability is general across Claw systems, but empirical evidence is limited to one model, one platform replica, and three domains

title: Photon-triplets for quantum optics generated by a phase-matched third-order difference-frequency mixing in a KTiOPO4 bulk crystal pumped at 532 nm

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires expertise in nonlinear quantum optics, phase-matching crystallography, single-photon detection, and coincidence measurement techniques
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The semiclassical model outperforming the quantum model by an order of magnitude is somewhat surprising; the triplet generation itself was expected given prior work by the same group
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Key experimental details like the transfer function are poorly constrained (2-20% range), data are not publicly available, and specific alignment procedures are described as 'very tricky' without detailed protocols
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Current flux rates are orders of magnitude too low for practical quantum information applications; significant engineering advances needed before deployment
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Only three measurements per data point, large uncertainties in both horizontal and vertical directions, and the critical transfer function parameter spans an order of magnitude
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: The semiclassical model could apply to other third-order parametric processes and crystals, but only one crystal type and one wavelength configuration were tested

title: ADAPT: Benchmarking Commonsense Planning under Unspecified Affordance Constraints

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: The technical approach combines existing components (LoRA, LLaVA, in-context learning) in a relatively standard way; the benchmark construction builds on ALFRED's pipeline with modifications
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The finding that existing methods fail under dynamic affordances is expected; the GPT-4o underperformance vs. fine-tuned models is mildly surprising but consistent with recent trends
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Code and partial data are released, benchmark is built on public AI2-THOR/ALFRED, but some implementation details (especially for baselines) require careful reconstruction
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Addresses a real-world need for robust household robots but restricted to simulation with limited affordance types; significant gap remains before real-world deployment
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Multiple baselines and ablations provided, but single-run evaluation without statistical tests, low absolute performance numbers, and oracle advantages for some baselines weaken evidence
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Only three affordance categories in one simulator with limited scene diversity; claims of platform-agnosticism are not empirically validated

title: Proximity Ferroelectricity Driven by Mobile High-Miller-Index Domain Walls

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires expertise in DFT, molecular dynamics, machine-learning potentials, and ferroelectric domain physics; combines multiple advanced computational techniques
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The inverse stability-mobility correlation for 180° DWs contradicts conventional wisdom from perovskite ferroelectrics; the role of high-index walls was previously overlooked
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Core computational methods described but key details in supplementary material not provided; DP model and training data availability unclear; standard codes (VASP, DeePMD-kit) are referenced
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly relevant to wurtzite ferroelectric memory device design; provides actionable design principles for heterostructure engineering to reduce coercive fields
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Multi-scale computational evidence is internally consistent and explains multiple experimental observations, but lacks direct experimental validation and involves extrapolation across timescales
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Mechanism demonstrated across AlN, GaN, and ZnO wurtzites with consistent trends; physical argument is general but limited to wurtzite-structured materials

title: The Power of Power Codes: New Classes of Easy Instances for the Linear Equivalence Problem

new_ratings.difficulty_reason: Requires solid background in algebraic coding theory, finite field arithmetic, and familiarity with the code equivalence problem landscape
new_ratings.surprisingness_reason: The existence of algebraic weak instances for LEP over arbitrary fields was not previously known and challenges the assumption that LEP is uniformly hard for q >= 5
new_ratings.reproducibility_reason: Algorithm 1 is clearly specified and constructions are explicit, but some probabilistic claims lack formal proofs and code is not provided
new_ratings.translational_potential_reason: Directly relevant to parameter selection for post-quantum cryptographic schemes based on code equivalence, though current parameters appear safe
new_ratings.evidence_strength_reason: Proof of Proposition 8 is sound but hull triviality is only experimentally verified; false positive analysis is heuristic; experiments are limited in scale
new_ratings.generalisability_reason: Framework covers all finite field sizes with multiple constructions, but the weak parameter ranges are restrictive and the approach may not extend to more general equivalence problems
